steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content: Rewrite the circle equation in standard form by completing the square for the $x$ terms and the $y$ terms. Then read the center from $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$.

steps.title: Complete the square for both variables
steps.content:
For $x^2 + 18x$: Take half of 18 to get 9, then square: $9^2 = 81$. Thus $x^2 + 18x = (x + 9)^2 - 81$.

For $y^2 + 12y$: Take half of 12 to get 6, then square: $6^2 = 36$. Thus $y^2 + 12y = (y + 6)^2 - 36$.

steps.title: Substitute and find the center in standard form
steps.content:
Substitute the completed squares into the original equation:
$$(x + 9)^2 - 81 + (y + 6)^2 - 36 = -36$$
Combine constants on the right:
$$(x + 9)^2 + (y + 6)^2 = -36 + 81 + 36 = 81$$
The standard form $(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$ has center $(h, k)$. Since $(x + 9)^2 = (x - (-9))^2$ and $(y + 6)^2 = (y - (-6))^2$, the center is $(-9, -6)$. That matches Choice B — $(-9,-6)$.
